name: mem-obsidian
description: Converts Claude Code sessions into structured, dense Obsidian notes.
instructions: When a user wraps up a session with keywords like 'done' or 'thanks', parse the session context. If academic, format with full formulas and step-by-step calculations and save to the coursework vault structure. If project-based, extract code snippets, system architecture changes, and decision logs, then save to the projects vault structure. Always present a dense markdown preview and ask for confirmation before saving.
